title: Add deep-linking to specific topics within modules (e.g., /m/security/oauth)
description: PLT-001 adds module-level routing. This extends to TOPIC-level deep linking: /m/security/oauth, /m/algorithms/bubble-sort. Each module wrapper reads topic slug from URL and passes to the module hook. For Security: map URL slugs to SecurityTopic type (oauth, jwt, aes, etc.).
acceptanceCriteria: URL /m/security/oauth loads Security module with OAuth topic active
Topic switching within module updates URL slug
Unknown slug shows module default state gracefully

title: Add keyboard shortcuts for modules 10-13 (Security, ML, Interview, KG have no shortcut)
description: activity-bar.tsx:53-56 shows Security, ML Design, Interview, Knowledge Graph with empty shortcut strings. Cmd+1-9 covers first 9. Add Cmd+0 for Security or Cmd+Shift+1-4 for modules 10-13.
acceptanceCriteria: All 13 modules accessible via keyboard shortcut
Shortcut shown in tooltip and KeyboardShortcutsDialog
No conflict with browser defaults

title: Create shared animation constants file and migrate modules to use it
description: lib/constants/motion.ts exists for dashboard but modules use hardcoded CSS values. Extend motion.ts with: SPRING_GENTLE, SPRING_SNAPPY, DURATION_FAST(150ms), DURATION_NORMAL(300ms), DURATION_SLOW(500ms). Migrate Security module as first adopter.
acceptanceCriteria: Shared animation constants in lib/constants/motion.ts
Security module uses shared constants instead of hardcoded 'duration-300'
Constants cover springs, durations, and easing curves

title: Wire up Security module to platform mastery/XP/streak tracking
description: useCrossModuleStore has moduleMastery and useProgressStore has XP/streaks, but Security module never calls updateModuleMastery() or addXP(). Wire up: viewing all steps of a topic â†’ updateModuleMastery('security', 'theory', 5). Completing challenge â†’ updateModuleMastery('security', 'practice', 10) + addXP(25). This makes the dashboard radar chart reflect actual Security module usage.
acceptanceCriteria: Stepping through all steps of a Security topic increases theory mastery
Dashboard radar chart shows non-zero Security mastery after usage
XP added for topic completion
Streak updated on daily Security module activity

title: Add Security-specific cross-module bridge definitions
description: bridge-types.ts defines AlgorithmToSystem, DataStructureToSystem, DatabaseToSystem payloads but zero Security bridge types. Add: SecurityToSystem (rate limitingâ†’API gateway, OAuthâ†’auth service, TLSâ†’load balancer), SecurityToNetworking (TLSâ†’HTTPS, CORSâ†’browser), SecurityToDatabase (password hashingâ†’storage). Plus intra-module: OAuthâ†’JWT, DHâ†’HTTPS, AESâ†’Encryption.
acceptanceCriteria: SecurityToSystem bridge type defined with 3+ connections
SecurityToNetworking bridge type with 2+ connections
RecommendedBridges shows Security-related suggestions when in Security module
Clicking a bridge navigates to the target module/topic

title: Enforce CSS variable usage in all SVG visualizations (design system lint rule)
description: globals.css defines 50+ CSS custom properties but SVG elements across modules use hardcoded hex. Security module alone has 40+ violations. Create lint rule or code convention doc: no fill/stroke hex in SVG â€” use style={{ fill: 'var(--surface)' }}. Provide migration guide per module.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration guide document for SVG CSS variable adoption
Security module SVGs migrated as reference implementation
ESLint custom rule or PR checklist to catch new violations

title: Add module-level bundle size monitoring (verify code splitting works)
description: All 14 module wrappers use dynamic(() => import(...), { ssr: false }) but no verification that chunks are reasonably sized. SecurityModule.tsx is 5169 lines, DataStructuresModule is 235k tokens. Run `ANALYZE=true next build` and document per-module chunk sizes. Set budgets: <200KB per module chunk. Flag violations.
acceptanceCriteria: Bundle analysis run with per-module chunk sizes documented
Each module chunk size < 200KB (or documented exception with plan)
CI check or manual process to monitor chunk growth
SecurityModule.tsx extraction (SEC-059-072) verified to reduce chunk size
